title: RPSC RAS 2026 — Rajasthan Administrative Services
meta_description: RPSC RAS 2026 — Rajasthan civil services exam for RAS, RPS, RTS officers. Prelims/Mains pattern, Rajasthan-specific syllabus, salary ₹56K-2L.

hero.one_liner: Rajasthan's prestigious state civil services exam — RAS officers (SDM equivalent), RPS (DSP equivalent), RTS (revenue officers) in state administration.

what_is_it: RPSC RAS (Rajasthan Public Service Commission — Rajasthan Administrative Service) is the combined competitive exam for Rajasthan state civil services, recruiting for three services: RAS (administrative), RPS (police), and RTS (revenue/taxation). RAS officers hold positions like SDM, ADM, District Collector (after promotion), Secretary-level. RPS officers become DSP (police), SP (after promotion), IG. RTS officers work in Commercial Taxes/GST. Three stages: Prelims (single GS paper), Mains (4 papers), Interview.

RAS Prelims is a single paper of 200 MCQs in 3 hours — GS only, no separate CSAT (unlike UPSC). This means pure GS testing, which is Rajasthan-specific. 30-40% questions test Rajasthan history (Rajput kingdoms, Mewar, Marwar, 1857 revolt), geography (Thar, Aravalli, rivers), art/culture (miniature paintings, folk music, forts), and current affairs. Non-Rajasthan candidates must invest 2-3 months learning Rajasthan deeply.

RAS is extremely competitive — latest cycle received 4+ lakh applications for ~500-900 vacancies (0.15% selection). Ajmer and Jaipur are coaching hubs. Success requires 12-18 month dedicated preparation. Starting salary at Level 10 (₹56,100) with DA and allowances reaching ₹80,000-1,00,000/month in Jaipur. RAS officers have significant administrative power at district level — promoting from SDM to Collector to Secretary offers career growth spanning 35+ years.

RAS 2026 notification expected March 2026, with Prelims in May 2026. Unlike UPSC which has CSAT, RAS Prelims is pure GS with no negative marking — you can attempt all 200 questions. Mains has 4 papers (GS-I, II, III + Hindi+English). Interview weightage: 60% from Mains+Interview combined, not just interview alone like UPSC.

key_details.label: Prelims
key_details.value: Single paper: GS (200 Qs, 200 marks, 3 hours). NO CSAT, no negative marking.

key_details.label: Mains
key_details.value: 4 papers: GS-I, GS-II, GS-III, Hindi+English (200 marks each, 800 total)

key_details.label: Rajasthan Focus
key_details.value: 30-40% questions on Rajasthan history, geography, art, culture, current affairs

exam_pattern.description: 200 MCQs on General Studies. Heavy Rajasthan focus (30-40%). No CSAT, no negative marking. Attempt all 200 questions.
exam_pattern.negative_marking: 1/3rd deducted per wrong answer (NOTE: Most recent RAS exams have negative marking — verify latest notification)

extra_sections.heading: Rajasthan GK — High-Weightage Topics
content:
Rajasthan History (30-40 Qs in Prelims)

Rajput dynasties: Chauhan empire (Delhi-Ajmer), Rathores (Jodhpur), Sisodias (Mewar/Udaipur), Kachhwahas (Jaipur). Mewar resistance against Mughals: Maharana Pratap, Battle of Haldighati (1576). 1857 in Rajasthan: Tatya Tope's role, various local uprisings.
Praja Mandal movements (pre-independence agitation). Integration of princely states (1949-1956) — merger with Union of India.
Early Rajasthan formation (1948-1956). Questions: 'Chauhan empire capital?' 'Haldighati battle year?' 'Maharana Pratap's father?' — memorize exact details.

Rajasthan Art & Culture (15-20 Qs)

Miniature painting schools: Kishangarh style (romantic), Bundi/Kota style, Mewar style, Jaipur style. Folk music: Maand (ancient form), Ghoomar (women's dance).
Folk dances: Kalbelia (snake charmer dance), Ghoomra. Handicrafts: Blue pottery (Jaipur), tie-dye (Jaipur-Sanganer), block printing (Sanganer).
Fairs: Pushkar Mela (camel fair), Teej Festival, Gangaur. Architecture: Hawa Mahal (Jaipur), City Palace, forts (Chittorgarh, Mehrangarh), stepwells (Baoris in Jodhpur).
Textiles: Jodhpur's famous fabrics, Ajmer's textiles.

Rajasthan Geography (20-25 Qs)

Thar Desert: Largest desert in Indian subcontinent, vegetation, adaptation, sand dunes. Aravalli Range: Ancient fold mountains, mineral resources, wildlife corridors.
Rivers: Chambal (only perennial in eastern Rajasthan), Luni (doesn't reach sea, loses in desert), Banas, Sutlej. Soil types: Sandy in west (Thar), red in east, black soil in south.
Minerals: Marble (Makrana), sandstone (Jodhpur), zinc/lead (Udaipur). Wildlife: Ranthambore (tigers), Desert National Park, Keoladeo Sanctuary.
Climate zones: Hot desert, semi-arid, sub-humid.

Rajasthan Current Affairs

Government schemes: Chiranjeevi Yojana (health insurance), Indira Gandhi Urban/Rural Employment Scheme. Budget highlights, major infrastructure projects, election results, important appointments.
State economy: Agriculture (wheat, mustard), dairy, tourism. Education: Central Universities, IIT, NIT presence.
Connectivity: NH expansion, rail projects, airport development.

extra_sections.heading: RAS vs UPSC CSE — Comparison
rows: Single GS paper (200 Qs, no CSAT)

preparation_tips: Rajasthan GK is NON-NEGOTIABLE — dedicate 30% of preparation time to Rajasthan-specific content. Primary resource: 'Rajasthan Adhyayan' by Kuldeep Publication (the standard RAS book), supplemented by L.P. Sharma for history and Lakshya Publication for GK. Read these books cover-to-cover multiple times. No shortcuts here.

RAS Prelims has 200 questions in 3 hours (54 seconds/question) — generous timing compared to UPSC's 2-hour pressure. Read each question carefully, think through answers. Don't rush. With good Rajasthan preparation + standard GS knowledge, scoring 120+ marks (cutoff ~95-100) is realistic. You need 60% to qualify.

Join RAS-focused coaching if possible — Utkarsh Classes, Spring Board Academy in Jaipur have strong RAS curricula. Their Rajasthan-specific modules and answer writing coaching save months. Alternatively, follow RAS YouTube channels and download Rajasthan government yearbooks from official sites.

Practice answer writing for Mains from month 4 onwards. RAS values crisp, factual answers with Rajasthan examples. Example: 'Discuss water scarcity in Rajasthan' — mention specific regions (Thar, Marwar), specific schemes (Indira Gandhi canal project), current data. Incorporate Rajasthan into general GS answers wherever relevant.

Take RAS-specific mocks from Ajmer coaching institutes. All-India UPSC mocks may not adequately test Rajasthan focus. RAS-specific mocks will expose Rajasthan knowledge gaps — critical for exam success.

faqs.question: RAS vs IAS — what's the difference in power and scope?
faqs.answer: RAS officers manage sub-divisions and districts WITHIN Rajasthan. IAS officers manage across India with international deputation options. RAS offers deeper local administrative experience; IAS offers broader exposure. In day-to-day district governance, RAS officers are equally powerful as IAS counterparts. Many Rajasthan collectors (district heads) are senior RAS officers. If you want to serve Rajasthan deeply, RAS is excellent. If wanting all-India exposure, IAS is better. Career satisfaction depends on personal preferences, not inherent superiority of either service.

faqs.question: RAS Prelims has no CSAT — confirmed?
faqs.answer: RAS Prelims traditionally has a single GS paper without a separate CSAT. However, General Mental Ability and Reasoning (10-15 questions) ARE part of the GS paper itself. So aptitude is tested, just not as a separate qualifying paper. Always verify the latest RPSC notification as patterns can change.

faqs.question: Can I prepare for RAS and UPSC CSE simultaneously?
faqs.answer: Yes. Overlap is 60-70%. National GS chapters are common. Additional effort for RAS: Rajasthan-specific GK (history, geography, art, culture — 2-3 months dedicated study). Ideal approach: Month 1-5 common GS, Month 6-8 Rajasthan focus + RAS Mains answer writing, Month 9-10 both Prelims practice. Many Rajasthan aspirants appear for both in the same year.

faqs.question: How is RAS Mains answer writing graded? Is it subjective?
faqs.answer: RAS Mains answers are graded on: (1) Content (factual accuracy, comprehensiveness), (2) Structure (logical flow, introduction-body-conclusion), (3) Legibility (handwriting clarity), (4) Length adherence (write within word limits). Unlike UPSC where evaluation can be subjective, RAS RPSC has published detailed answer key guidance and model answers. Quality answers with Rajasthan examples score 60-70% consistently. Mediocre answers score 30-40%. Top answers (rare) score 80-90%. Practice writing 1-2 answers per day from month 4 onwards for proficiency.

faqs.question: What resources should I use for Rajasthan-specific GK preparation?
faqs.answer: Primary resources: (1) 'Rajasthan Adhyayan' by Kuldeep Publication — the standard RAS textbook covering history, geography, art, culture comprehensively. Read this 2-3 times. (2) 'Rajasthan History' by L.P. Sharma — focused on Rajasthan history, especially Rajput dynasties and modern periods. (3) Rajasthan Government Yearbook — official publication with current state data, schemes, budget, administration structure. Download from rajasthan.gov.in. (4) Lakshya Publication GK books — condensed Rajasthan GK focused on exam-relevant topics. (5) Current affairs from: Raj Express, Amar Ujala, Dainik Bhaskar (Rajasthan edition), and Rajasthan government press releases. (6) YouTube channels dedicated to RAS (Utkarsh Classes, Spring Board Academy — Jaipur-based). These resources collectively cover 100% of RAS exam scope.

syllabus.subject: General Studies Paper I
syllabus.topics: Indian history, geography, polity, economics. Rajasthan-specific: Rajasthan history (Rajput kingdoms, Mewar, Marwar), Rajasthan geography (Thar desert, Aravalli), Rajasthan art & culture (miniature paintings, folk music, fairs), Rajasthan government schemes (Chiranjeevi)

syllabus.subject: CSAT / General Aptitude (Qualifying)
syllabus.topics: Comprehension, logical reasoning, analytical ability, basic numeracy, data interpretation, decision making

syllabus.subject: Rajasthan Specific GK
syllabus.topics: Rajasthan history (Rajput kingdoms, Mewar, Marwar), Rajasthan geography (Thar desert, Aravalli), Rajasthan art & culture (miniature paintings, folk music, fairs), Rajasthan government schemes (Chiranjeevi)

study_plan.tasks: Read NCERTs for History, Geography, Polity. Start Rajasthan state GK book. Study state-specific history, geography, culture, and current schemes. Daily newspaper reading for current affairs.
Laxmikanth Polity, Ramesh Singh Economy. Deep dive into Rajasthan-specific topics — this is what separates State PSC from UPSC. Start answer writing practice for Mains (250 words).
Solve last 10 years RPSCRAS papers. Weekly full mocks. Focus on Rajasthan current affairs and state government policies. Interview preparation alongside.
